pi_items.source: Task specification requires creation at this path
Task specification requires creation at this path
Task description specifies 8 commercial traffic vessels (container, cruise, tanker, ferry, ferry, bulk, naval, tug)
Solent extends from approximately 50.6°N (south, near Isle of Wight/Bembridge 50.6863°N) to 50.9°N (north). Web search confirmed Bembridge coordinates 50.6863°N, -1.0828°W as eastern boundary.
Solent bounds extend northward to approximately 50.9°N. Web search confirmed Solent spans about 20 miles east-west. Northern boundary covers Southampton Water area north of Calshot Spit.
Solent western boundary at Hurst Spit; task bounds long_min -1.55W cover the Hurst Spit area appropriately for Solent's extent westward.
Solent eastern boundary extends to Bembridge (50.6863°N, -1.0828°W). Task bound long_max -1.00W provides reasonable eastern coverage.
Web search confirmed RAF Calshot coordinates: 50.8183°N, -1.3050°W and Calshot Spit: 50.8167°N, -1.3064°W. Task value 50.82°N is accurate to 2 decimal places.
Web search confirmed RAF Calshot coordinates: 50.8183°N, -1.3050°W. Task value -1.31°W is accurate to 2 decimal places.
Task-defined tolerance; reasonable for ship positioning in simulator (approximately 5.5 km at this latitude)
VHF (Very High Frequency) marine radio is standard maritime communication. Web search confirmed VHF channels 12 and 14 are used for Southampton VTS and Portsmouth operations.
VHF channel terminology is standard maritime practice. Task asks for keywords 'channel', '12', '14' which are specific channels used in Solent area.

Web search confirmed VHF Channel 12 is Southampton VTS (Vessel Traffic Service) mandatory reporting channel for vessels in Solent/Southampton Water. All vessels >20m entering East Solent must report on Channel 12.
Web search confirmed VHF Channel 14 is used by Portsmouth Harbour Radio and Portsmouth International Pilots in the Solent region.

Web search confirmed deep draft vessels are a significant concern in Southampton Water adjacent to the Solent. Deep draft shipping executes complicated maneuvering near Calshot Spit due to shallow waters and obstacles like Bramble bank.
Web search confirmed deep draft vessels navigate Southampton Water. 'Draft' refers to how deep a ship sits in water, critical for Solent navigation planning.
Web search results reference crossing traffic and collision avoidance in Solent. Task mentions 'crossing traffic conflicts' in description.
Task description explicitly mentions 'crossing traffic conflicts' as part of VTS watch handover log content to be identified.
Task requires '1 Container ship (inbound)' as one of 8 vessels. Container ships are common commercial traffic in Southampton Water.
Task requires two ferry vessels: '1 Car ferry (crossing Southampton Water)' and '1 IoW ferry (crossing Solent)'. Ferries are primary cross-Solent traffic.

privileged_info_summary: The VTS Traffic Density Exercise task is set in the Solent, a real 20-mile strait between Hampshire and the Isle of Wight in southern England. Geographic metadata is accurate: Calshot Spit is verified at 50.8183°N, -1.3050°W (task claims 50.82°N, -1.31°W); Solent bounds (50.65-50.90°N, -1.55 to -1.00°W) correctly encompass the water body from Hurst Spit west to Bembridge east. VHF Channels 12 and 14 are confirmed as mandatory for Southampton VTS and Portsmouth operations respectively. Deep draft vessel handling is a verified real-world navigation challenge in adjacent Southampton Water. All required keywords (VHF, channels, deep/draft, crossing/conflict, container, ferry) are grounded in actual Solent maritime operations. The scenario data is synthetic (user-created), but based on genuine geographic and operational parameters.
